title: Bacterial Transformation Protocol
summary: Protocol for transforming competent bacterial cells with DNA using heat shock, recovery, and plating on selective LB agar.
summary_en: Protocol for transforming competent bacterial cells with DNA using heat shock, recovery, and plating on selective LB agar.

steps.text: Take competent cells out of -80°C and thaw on ice (approximately 20-30 mins).
steps.text_en: Take competent cells out of -80°C and thaw on ice (approximately 20-30 mins).
steps.verbatim: Take competent cells out of -80°C and thaw on ice (approximately 20-30 mins).

steps.text: Remove agar plates (containing the appropriate antibiotic) from storage at 4°C and let warm up to room temperature and then (optional) incubate in 37°C incubator.
steps.text_en: Remove agar plates (containing the appropriate antibiotic) from storage at 4°C and let warm up to room temperature and then (optional) incubate in 37°C incubator.
steps.verbatim: Remove agar plates (containing the appropriate antibiotic) from storage at 4°C and let warm up to room temperature and then (optional) incubate in 37°C incubator.

steps.text: Mix 1 - 5 µl of DNA (usually 10 pg - 100 ng) into 20-50 µL of competent cells in a microcentrifuge or falcon tube. GENTLY mix by flicking the bottom of the tube with your finger a few times.
steps.text_en: Mix 1 - 5 µl of DNA (usually 10 pg - 100 ng) into 20-50 µL of competent cells in a microcentrifuge or falcon tube. GENTLY mix by flicking the bottom of the tube with your finger a few times.
hazards: GENTLY mix by flicking; do not vortex
hazards_en: GENTLY mix by flicking; do not vortex
steps.verbatim: Mix 1 - 5 µl of DNA (usually 10 pg - 100 ng) into 20-50 µL of competent cells in a microcentrifuge or falcon tube. GENTLY mix by flicking the bottom of the tube with your finger a few times.

steps.text: Incubate the competent cell/DNA mixture on ice for 20-30 mins.
steps.text_en: Incubate the competent cell/DNA mixture on ice for 20-30 mins.
steps.verbatim: Incubate the competent cell/DNA mixture on ice for 20-30 mins.

steps.text: Heat shock each transformation tube by placing the bottom 1/2 to 2/3 of the tube into a 42°C water bath for 30-60 secs (45 secs is usually ideal, but this varies depending on the competent cells you are using).
steps.text_en: Heat shock each transformation tube by placing the bottom 1/2 to 2/3 of the tube into a 42°C water bath for 30-60 secs (45 secs is usually ideal, but this varies depending on the competent cells you are using).
hazards: do not exceed 30-60 secs; timing varies by competent cell type
hazards_en: do not exceed 30-60 secs; timing varies by competent cell type

gaps.question: What is the optimal heat shock time for the specific competent cells being used (30-60 secs, typically 45 secs)?

steps.verbatim: Heat shock each transformation tube by placing the bottom 1/2 to 2/3 of the tube into a 42°C water bath for 30-60 secs (45 secs is usually ideal, but this varies depending on the competent cells you are using).

steps.text: Put the tubes back on ice for 2 min.
steps.text_en: Put the tubes back on ice for 2 min.
steps.verbatim: Put the tubes back on ice for 2 min.

steps.text: Add 250-1,000 µl LB or SOC media (without antibiotic) to the bacteria.
steps.text_en: Add 250-1,000 µl LB or SOC media (without antibiotic) to the bacteria.
steps.verbatim: Add 250-1,000 µl LB or SOC media (without antibiotic) to the bacteria and grow in 37°C shaking incubator for 45 min.

steps.text: Grow in 37°C shaking incubator for 45 min.
steps.text_en: Grow in 37°C shaking incubator for 45 min.
steps.verbatim: Add 250-1,000 µl LB or SOC media (without antibiotic) to the bacteria and grow in 37°C shaking incubator for 45 min.

steps.text: Plate some or all of the transformation onto a 10 cm LB agar plate containing the appropriate antibiotic.
steps.text_en: Plate some or all of the transformation onto a 10 cm LB agar plate containing the appropriate antibiotic.

gaps.question: How much of the transformation should be plated (some or all)?

steps.verbatim: Plate some or all of the transformation onto a 10 cm LB agar plate containing the appropriate antibiotic.

open_parameters.question: What amount of DNA to use (1-5 µl, usually 10 pg - 100 ng)?
What volume of competent cells to use (20-50 µL)?
Which recovery media to use (LB or SOC) and what volume (250-1,000 µl)?
How much of the transformation to plate (some or all)?
Which antibiotic is appropriate for the selection?
